zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Baskerville於1439年出生在英國賀福爾波吉爾時，她的父親Sir Ralph Baskerville29歲，母親Anne Blackett27歲。她於1460年與Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire在英國賀福爾特尼--也結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和12個女兒的父母。她去世於1488年在英國賀福爾易亞迪斯利享年49歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾易亞迪斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Baskerville于1439年出生在英国賀福爾波吉爾时，她的父亲Sir Ralph Baskerville29岁，母亲Anne Blackett27岁。她于1460年与Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire在英国賀福爾特尼--也结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和12个女儿的父母。她去世于1488年在英国賀福爾易亞迪斯利享年49岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾易亞迪斯利。

nameMeanings.name: Baskerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Baskerville was born in 1439, in Burghill, Herefordshire, England, her father, Sir Ralph Baskerville, was 29 and her mother, Anne Blackett, was 27. She married Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire in 1460, in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 12 daughters. She died in 1488, in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Baskerville est née en 1439, à Burghill, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Ralph Baskerville, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Anne Blackett, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire en 1460, à Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 12 filles. Elle est décédée en 1488, à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.Stow on the Wold, Gloucestershire, Engla: Stow on the Wold, Gloucestershire, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Baskerville 1439 in Burghill, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Ralph Baskerville 29 und ihre Mutter Anne Blackett 27. Sie hat 1460 in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 12 Töchter. Sie starb 1488 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Baskervilleが1439年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブルギルで生まれた時，父Sir Ralph Baskervilleは29歳，母Anne Blackettは27歳であった。1460年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィトニー-オン-ウイでSir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と12人の娘の親であった。彼女は1488年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアルディスリーで49歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアルディスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Baskerville(이)가 1439년 영국 허퍼드실르 버그힐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Ralph Baskerville(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Blackett(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 허퍼드실르 휘트니--w예에서 Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1488년 영국 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Baskerville nasceu em 1439, em Burghill, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Ralph Baskerville, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Anne Blackett, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire em 1460, em Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 12 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1488, em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Stow on the Wold, Gloucestershire, Engla: стоу он т волд, глоусестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Baskerville родилась в 1439 г. в местности бургилл, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Ralph Baskerville было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Blackett, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Simon Milbourne Sheriff Of Herefordshire в 1460 г. в местности уитни-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 12 дочерей. Она умерла в 1488 г. в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия.
